title: SQLite store is in-memory (`:memory:`) over a pooled sql.DB — non-persistent and unsafe under concurrency
evidence: main.go:15 `NewBookStore(":memory:")`; database.go:19-44 opens a database/sql pool with no `SetMaxOpenConns(1)`. With mattn/go-sqlite3 each pooled connection to `:memory:` is a SEPARATE database, so under concurrent requests a query can land on a connection whose `books` table/data is absent ("no such table: books" / lost writes). Data is also lost on every restart. Tests pass only because they run sequentially on one reused connection.
suggestion: Use a file-backed DSN (e.g. `books.db`) or `file::memory:?cache=shared`, and call `db.SetMaxOpenConns(1)` for in-memory use to keep all access on one connection.

title: Unused exported dispatcher methods HandleBooks / HandleBookByID
evidence: handlers.go:34 HandleBooks and handlers.go:46 HandleBookByID are defined but never referenced — main.go:25-61 wires routes with inline closures instead.
suggestion: Delete the two dead methods or route through them from main.go to remove the duplication.

title: Test coverage 50.3% — router wiring and main() are untested
evidence: scores.json test_coverage=0.503; main.go:12-68 (mux setup, ID parsing, method dispatch) and the unused HandleBooks/HandleBookByID have no test exercising them. Handlers are tested directly, bypassing the mux.
suggestion: Add httptest.Server or ServeMux-level tests covering path parsing (`/books/`, non-numeric id -> 400) and method-not-allowed dispatch.
